title: AFI Pipeline Manifest v1 (FACTORY-CONTRACT — canonical analyst-configurable pipeline topology)
description: The canonical pipeline topology contract for AFI analyst-configurable pipelines (afi.pipeline.v1). A pipeline manifest is a DECLARATIVE, deterministic description of a directed acyclic analysis graph: nodes (plugin-backed analysis stages in the seven governed categories) and edges (dataflow, optionally condition-gated by a schema-validated predicate tree). nodes/edges is the ONE topology representation: there is deliberately NO enrichmentNodes map and NO requiredNodes list (the retired ungoverned drafts' three overlapping mechanisms are collapsed into this single one). The manifest binds plugins by pluginId+pluginVersion ONLY — binding to code happens in the consuming runtime's build-time plugin registry; no filesystem paths are representable. Exactly one node of category 'scorer' terminates every admissible graph (the single scoring seam); graph-semantic invariants JSON Schema draft-07 cannot express are governed contract constraints in x-afiConstraints, enforced by the accompanying vector/tooling tests.
$comment: BOUNDARY (FACTORY-CONTRACT): schema/contract only — NO executor, NO plugin loading, NO runtime wiring, NO storage. The consuming runtime (afi-reactor pipelineRunner lineage) and the authoring factory (afi-factory) validate manifests against this schema PLUS the x-afiConstraints graph semantics before execution. node.config is an open object HERE and is validated downstream against the bound plugin's paramsSchema (afi.analysis-plugin.v1). Canonical manifest hashing (manifestHash) is specified by schemas/hashing/canonical-json-hashing.v1.md: description and metadata are volatile/annotational and are EXCLUDED from canonical hash material.
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/factory-configurable-pipelines-v1 — authorizes the canonical V1 contract family (pipeline, pipeline-template, analysis-plugin, analyst-strategy-config/registration, provider-strategy-binding, composition-ref, and the scored-signal evidence contract family — current version afi.scored-signal-evidence.v3 per EV3-GOV) and the retirement of the superseded ungoverned drafts

reports/afi-configurable-dag-live-runtime-reconciliation.md §5/§9 N7 — canonical topology contract minted FRESH as afi.pipeline.v1 (never a mutation of the retired draft); ONE topology mechanism chosen (nodes/edges); node categories aligned to the code-canonical five analysis categories + merge + scorer

afi-governance/decisions/lifecycle-v0.1.md (LIFE-GOV) D-LIFE-1/D-LIFE-4 — one scoring seam: only the scorer stage effects VALIDATED→SCORED; no enrichment node performs lifecycle transitions (realized structurally: exactly one non-bypassable scorer sink)

afi-governance/decisions/persistence-v0.1.md (MONGO-GOV) D-MONGO-9 — deterministic replay/verify: joins must be deterministic by construction (join.merge conflictRule mandatory), conditions are data (predicate trees), never code
afi-governance/decisions/object-identity-v0.1.md (OBJ-GOV) D-OBJ-6 — two-axis versioning pattern: per-object schema-id const afi.pipeline.v1 + explicit pipelineVersion

x-afiConstraints.uniqueNodeIds: GRAPH-SEMANTIC (not JSON-Schema-draft-07-enforceable): every nodes[].id MUST be unique within the manifest. Enforced by the manifest tooling/tests (see tests/pipeline-schema-validation.test.ts), never assumed.
x-afiConstraints.knownEndpoints: GRAPH-SEMANTIC: entry and every edges[].from / edges[].to MUST name a declared node id; self-edges are forbidden.
x-afiConstraints.acyclicity: GRAPH-SEMANTIC: the edge set MUST be acyclic (Kahn). A manifest with a cycle is inadmissible regardless of schema validity.
x-afiConstraints.singleScorer: GRAPH-SEMANTIC: exactly ONE node of category 'scorer'. The schema structurally requires AT LEAST one (nodes 'contains'); the at-most-one cap is enforced by tooling/tests. The scorer is the single scoring seam (LIFE-GOV D-LIFE-1).
x-afiConstraints.scorerTerminality: GRAPH-SEMANTIC: the scorer MUST be a sink (no outgoing edges), MUST be reachable from entry, and MUST NOT be bypassable: every node is reachable from entry and the scorer is the ONLY sink — so every path from entry that reaches any sink reaches the scorer. A non-scorer sink is inadmissible.
x-afiConstraints.joinDeclaration: GRAPH-SEMANTIC: any node with MORE THAN ONE incoming edge MUST declare join:{policy:'all', merge:{strategy, conflictRule}} — deterministic by construction; nodes with zero or one incoming edge MUST NOT declare join. A conflictRule of the form 'prefer:<nodeId>' MUST name one of that node's parent nodes.
x-afiConstraints.deterministicConditions: Edge conditions are schema-validated predicate trees over JSON-pointer-style paths into VALIDATED node outputs (/nodes/<nodeId>/output/...) or pipeline context (/context/...). They are pure data — deterministic given the same inputs. Code strings, expression languages, and unknown operators are structurally rejected (the condition property admits only the predicate-tree shape).
x-afiConstraints.configValidatedDownstream: node.config is an open object at this layer and MUST be validated by the consuming factory/runtime against the bound plugin's paramsSchema (afi.analysis-plugin.v1, resolved by pluginId+pluginVersion) before the manifest is accepted for execution.
x-afiConstraints.pluginBinding: The manifest binds plugins by pluginId+pluginVersion ONLY. Binding to code happens in the consuming runtime's build-time plugin registry; filesystem paths are not representable in this contract.
x-afiConstraints.providerInstanceRef: OPTIONAL per-node non-secret reference (providerInstanceId + recordVersion) to an afi.provider-instance.v1 record (PBF-GOV D-PBF-4/D-PBF-7). It NEVER carries a credential value, secret, endpoint URL, or CredentialRef payload; additionalProperties:false on the ref object leaves secrets nowhere to live. Resolution (provider/category/adapter compatibility + least-privilege credential resolution) happens BELOW the node in the runtime provider-adapter layer, not in this contract. Being nested node data, a present ref is INCLUDED in canonical manifest hash material; absent (the common case for keyless in-registry plugin nodes) it changes no existing manifest hash.
x-afiConstraints.oneRepresentation: nodes/edges is the ONE topology representation. The retired drafts' parallel mechanisms (enrichmentNodes map, requiredNodes list) are structurally rejected by additionalProperties:false and MUST NOT be reintroduced.
x-afiConstraints.hashMaterial: Canonical manifest hashing (manifestHash, domain tag afi.factory.pipeline-manifest) follows schemas/hashing/canonical-json-hashing.v1.md; description and metadata are excluded from canonical hash material. metadata is free-form and NON-AUTHORITATIVE: nothing in it may alter execution.

schema.description: Schema-id version of the pipeline manifest (OBJ-GOV D-OBJ-6 axis (a)).

pipelineId.description: Stable identifier of the pipeline topology (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens). Registry key: one file per pipelineId+pipelineVersion under registries/pipelines/.

pipelineVersion.description: Semantic version of this pipeline topology, WITH v prefix (v{major}.{minor}.{patch}). Any topology change requires a new version — never a mutation.

description.description: OPTIONAL human-readable description. Annotational only — EXCLUDED from canonical hash material.

entry.description: Node id of the single entry node. MUST name a declared node (x-afiConstraints.knownEndpoints); every node must be reachable from it.

nodes.description: The analysis stages of the graph. Node ids MUST be unique (x-afiConstraints.uniqueNodeIds). Structurally requires at least one 'scorer' node; exactly-one is a governed graph constraint.

edges.description: Directed dataflow edges. The edge set MUST be acyclic and MUST make the scorer the only sink reachable from entry (x-afiConstraints).

metadata.description: OPTIONAL free-form annotations (author, notes, provenance of authorship). NON-AUTHORITATIVE: nothing here may alter execution; EXCLUDED from canonical hash material.

id.description: Unique node id within the manifest (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens).

category.description: Governed node category: the five code-canonical analysis categories (technical|pattern|sentiment|news|aiMl) plus 'merge' (structured join stage) and 'scorer' (the single scoring seam). No other categories exist in v1.

pluginId.description: Id of the afi.analysis-plugin.v1 manifest implementing this node. Resolved against the runtime's build-time plugin registry — never a filesystem path.

pluginVersion.description: Exact semver (NO v prefix) of the bound plugin manifest.

config.description: OPTIONAL node configuration. Open object at this layer; validated DOWNSTREAM against the bound plugin's paramsSchema (x-afiConstraints.configValidatedDownstream).

timeoutMs.description: OPTIONAL per-node execution timeout in milliseconds (>= 1). Absent means the plugin's defaultTimeoutMs (or no timeout if that too is absent).

maxRetries.description: OPTIONAL maximum retry count after the first attempt (>= 0).

retryDelayMs.description: OPTIONAL base delay between retries in milliseconds.

backoff.description: OPTIONAL retry backoff policy applied to retryDelayMs.

critical.description: Whether a failure of this node aborts the pipeline. Defaults to TRUE (fail-fast). Only critical:false nodes may declare failurePolicy 'degrade'.

failurePolicy.description: OPTIONAL failure policy: 'abort' fails the whole run; 'degrade' records the degradation and continues to the join — allowed ONLY when critical is explicitly false (bound structurally by if/then below).

resourceLimits.description: OPTIONAL operational resource limits for the node (open object; operational hints only, never scoring semantics).

join.description: REQUIRED on (and only on) nodes with more than one incoming edge (x-afiConstraints.joinDeclaration). Declares the deterministic all-parents join and merge rule.

providerInstanceRef.description: OPTIONAL non-secret reference to a versioned provider instance (afi.provider-instance.v1) that supplies this category node's implementation (x-afiConstraints.providerInstanceRef). Identity + version ONLY — NEVER a credential value, secret, endpoint URL, or CredentialRef payload. Resolved BELOW the node by the runtime provider-adapter layer against deployment-local provider-instance configuration. Absent for in-registry keyless plugin nodes.

Non-secret, versioned reference to an afi.provider-instance.v1 record. Carries identity + version ONLY — never a credential value, secret, endpoint URL, or CredentialRef payload (PBF-GOV D-PBF-4/D-PBF-7). The runtime provider-adapter layer resolves the instance BELOW the node, validates category/adapter compatibility, and resolves the authorized credential (if any) via a least-privilege SecretResolver. Not resolved in this contract.

providerInstanceId.description: Stable id of the referenced afi.provider-instance.v1 record (deployment-local; not resolved in this contract).

recordVersion.description: Exact semver of the referenced provider-instance record, version-pinned for deterministic composition.

policy.description: v1 fixes the join policy to 'all': the node runs when ALL non-optional parents have completed (optional parents that were skipped/degraded are joined as absent).

merge.description: Deterministic merge of parent outputs — deterministic by construction: strategy + conflictRule are both mandatory, so branch completion order can never change the merged value (MONGO-GOV D-MONGO-9).

strategy.description: 'namespace-by-node': parent outputs are merged under their node ids (no key collisions possible). 'declared-fields': parent outputs contribute the fields declared by their plugin outputSchemaRef; collisions resolve via conflictRule.

conflictRule.description: 'error': any field collision fails the run. 'prefer:<nodeId>': collisions resolve to the named parent node's value; <nodeId> MUST be one of this node's parents (x-afiConstraints.joinDeclaration).

from.description: Source node id (must be declared in nodes).

to.description: Target node id (must be declared in nodes).

fromPort.description: OPTIONAL named output port on the source node.

toPort.description: OPTIONAL named input port on the target node.

condition.description: OPTIONAL activation predicate: the edge fires only when the predicate evaluates true over validated node outputs / pipeline context. Pure data (predicate tree) — code strings are structurally rejected.

optional.description: OPTIONAL marker that this edge's parent is optional for a downstream join: if the parent was skipped (condition false) or degraded, the join proceeds without it instead of waiting/failing.

path.description: JSON-pointer-style path into validated node outputs (/nodes/<nodeId>/output/...) or pipeline context (/context/...). Non-empty segments only; never an expression.

value.description: Scalar comparison operand (objects/arrays are not comparable operands in v1).

predicate.description: Deterministic predicate tree. Exactly one operator per object: all/any (conjunction/disjunction over sub-predicates), not, exists(path), eq/ne (scalar equality), gt/gte/lt/lte (numeric ordering), in (path, values[]). Unknown operators and code strings are structurally rejected.
